steps.title: Identify what the passage calls surprising
steps.content: The passage states scientists expected marsquakes to originate from all over the planet, but the InSight data showed the opposite — every marsquake started from the same single location. That direct contradiction between expectation and finding is what the passage marks as the surprising discovery.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — The surface temperature of Mars has been rising.: Not mentioned in the passage; unrelated to marsquake origins. ✗
  • Choice B — There were different types of seismic waves causing marsquakes.: The passage does not discuss wave types; this is not the stated surprise. ✗
  • Choice C — NASA's InSight lander collected less data than scientists had expected.: Contradicts the passage, which describes data leading to a discovery, not a data shortage. ✗
  • Choice D — All the marsquakes started from the same location on the planet.: Restates precisely what the passage identifies as the surprising finding, directly contradicting the scientists' expectation of widespread origins. ✓
That is the correct answer.
